message:
Improve BeadsView graph rendering, script detection, and layout

Multiple targeted improvements to the force-graph beads viewer:

Script loading robustness:
- Replaced the one-shot useEffect that checked window globals
  once on mount with a setInterval(100ms) polling loop that
  repeatedly checks for window.initSqlJs, window.d3, and
  window.ForceGraph until all three are detected, then auto-clears
  the interval. This fixes the race condition on navigation-back
  where the Script onLoad callbacks have already fired but the
  component remounts fresh—the polling picks up the already-loaded
  globals within 100ms instead of requiring a page reload.

Graph node rendering:
- Node val formula changed from (5 - priority) to
  (5 - priority) * 2 + 2, giving higher-priority beads more
  visual weight in the physics simulation
- Canvas node size reduced from (5 - priority) * 2 + 4 to
  (5 - priority) * 1.5 + 3 for tighter visual density
- Added glow effect via ctx.shadowColor and ctx.shadowBlur
  (10 / globalScale) using each node's status color, creating
  a neon halo that scales inversely with zoom
- Label visibility threshold lowered from globalScale > 1.5 to
  > 1.2, so bead IDs appear at lower zoom levels
- Label font size reduced from 12 to 10 (divided by globalScale)
- Label opacity reduced from 0.8 to 0.7 for subtlety
- Shadow explicitly cleared (shadowBlur = 0) before drawing text
  to prevent glow from bleeding into labels

Layout and sizing:
- Graph height now uses containerRef.current.clientHeight || 650
  instead of a hardcoded 650, allowing the container to drive
  sizing when CSS provides a height
- ResizeObserver callback now syncs both width AND height
  (previously only synced width)

Render structure:
- Reverted from early-return loading pattern back to unified
  ternary ({loading ? <spinner/> : <content/>}) with Script tags
  rendered at the top level outside the conditional, ensuring
  vendor scripts persist in the DOM during both loading and
  loaded states and their onLoad callbacks fire reliably
- Added text-left to telemetry stat baselines and Priority_Ledger
  header for consistent left-alignment
- Cleaned up excessive indentation in the Synapse_Descriptor
  modal section where Streamdown renders bead descriptions

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
